codeReview.summary: The original transport-hardening commit correctly exposed timeout/compression budgets in runtime diagnostics and wired Fastify request/connection timeouts, but it initially failed the compression requirement because gzip eligibility depended on a missing Content-Length header and its test allowed uncompressed success. The follow-up fix commit 88325d90f4cf183d8beec817111e3636ea9e71e5 addresses both blocking findings by computing threshold eligibility from the actual payload bytes and by requiring gzip for an above-threshold JSON response while still asserting decompressed-body equivalence. Current transport code now preserves unrelated endpoint bodies while allowing negotiated transport headers on non-hot endpoints, which matches the feature contract.

summary: Pass after rerun. I reviewed the original feature diff/transcript plus the compression-threshold-enforcement fix and confirmed the previous blocking compression/test-enforcement issues are resolved: timeout budgets remain externally discoverable, threshold-based gzip now uses actual payload size, and unrelated endpoint body contracts remain intact apart from negotiated transport headers.
